summary: Ingredient-Based Recipe Recommendation System with Data Analytics (SpiceAI) — a web app that takes user-entered ingredients, fetches Indian recipes via the Spoonacular API, displays recipe cards with details (nutrition, ingredients, steps), and shows a calorie analytics chart. The user wants a beautiful, enhanced UI version of their existing HTML/CSS/JS codebase.
architectural_notes: Uses Spoonacular API (findByIngredients + recipe information endpoints) with API key in frontend JS
Three files: index.html, style.css, script.js — no backend
Chart.js for calorie analytics bar chart; Lottie for loading animation
Cuisine filter locked to Indian recipes
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Wide cinematic hero banner showing a vibrant spread of colorful Indian spices, fresh vegetables including tomato onion paneer and coriander, and decorative copper cooking pots on a warm wooden surface with rich warm golden lighting (spiceai-hero.dim_1440x500.png)
- Seamless repeating tile pattern of small Indian spice illustrations including mustard seeds chili peppers cumin and coriander leaves on a warm cream background suitable as a subtle texture (spice-pattern.dim_400x400.png)
- Horizontal wordmark logo with a small stylized chili pepper icon beside bold text SpiceAI on a transparent background using saffron and crimson tones (spiceai-logo.dim_120x40.png)

existing_features.title: Build a full-page React application titled SpiceAI — Ingredient-Based Recipe Recommendation System with Data Analytics. The app must have a hero header section with the SpiceAI brand name (with a curry bowl emoji), the full project subtitle INGREDIENT-BASED RECIPE RECOMMENDATION SYSTEM WITH DATA ANALYTICS, and a visually rich background using the hero banner image (spiceai-hero.dim_1440x500.png) as a backdrop with a warm gradient overlay.

Implement an ingredient search section with a styled input field where users can type ingredients separated by commas (e.g., tomato, onion, paneer). Include a prominent Find Recipes button. When submitted, call the Spoonacular API: GET https://api.spoonacular.com/recipes/findByIngredients?ingredients={ingredients}&number=5&cuisine=Indian&apiKey=f1a04579d22649258b5b03a04d119c6f. Show a loading spinner with text 'Cooking delicious Indian recipes...' while the request is in progress. If the input is empty, show a validation message.

Display recipe search results as a responsive grid of recipe cards. Each card must show: the recipe image, recipe title, a green badge for number of used ingredients, an orange badge for number of missing ingredients, and a View Recipe button. Cards must have hover lift/scale effects. Use React Query to manage API fetch state.

Implement a recipe detail view. When View Recipe is clicked, fetch from: GET https://api.spoonacular.com/recipes/{id}/information?includeNutrition=true&apiKey=f1a04579d22649258b5b03a04d119c6f. Display: recipe title, image, a nutrition panel with styled stat cards for Calories (kcal), Protein (g), Fat (g), and Carbohydrates (g), a full ingredient list, and numbered step-by-step cooking instructions. Include a Back to Results button to return to the recipe list.

Add a Data Analytics section below the recipe results. Render a bar chart comparing Calorie content (kcal) for each recipe viewed in the current session, labelled Ingredient Analytics — Calorie Comparison. Also render a doughnut chart showing the macronutrient breakdown (Protein, Fat, Carbohydrates) for the most recently viewed recipe. Use Recharts or react-chartjs-2. Both charts must be styled with the app color theme and update dynamically as the user views more recipes.

Apply a warm Indian-cuisine-inspired visual theme across the entire application. Use a saffron-to-deep-crimson color palette (primary #E63946, accent #F4A261, warm background #FFF8F0, secondary green #2D6A4F). Load Poppins font from Google Fonts for all text. Use rounded cards with soft shadows, generous spacing, and smooth transitions. Set a full-width layout with a centered 1200px max-width container. Apply the spice-pattern texture (spice-pattern.dim_400x400.png) as a subtle repeating tile on section backgrounds.

Render the generated hero banner image (spiceai-hero.dim_1440x500.png) and spice pattern texture (spice-pattern.dim_400x400.png) and logo (spiceai-logo.dim_120x40.png) as static assets served from frontend/public/assets/generated. Reference them in the hero header background, section background tiling, and navbar logo respectively.

Add a sticky navigation bar at the top with the SpiceAI logo (spiceai-logo.dim_120x40.png) on the left and anchor links to Search, Recipes, and Analytics sections on the right. The navbar should start semi-transparent with a frosted-glass effect and transition to a solid warm background color on scroll.

new_feature_requests.summary: Enhanced beautiful UI for SpiceAI recipe recommendation website with improved styling, layout, and visual appeal
